findings.message: Tautological assertion: `assert s is s` will pass for any object regardless of Settings' behaviour. This test cannot fail and provides zero coverage of the code under test.
findings.suggestion: Replace with a meaningful assertion about Settings' actual behaviour (e.g., assert a default attribute value like `assert Settings().environment == 'dev'`), or remove the test entirely.

findings.message: Over-mocked / tautology: the test patches `Settings.resolved_log_level` to return 'MOCKED' and then asserts the patched value equals 'MOCKED'. This asserts on the mock itself — the real `resolved_log_level` property is never exercised, so the test will pass even if the production logic is broken or deleted.
findings.suggestion: Remove the patch and test the real property by varying inputs (e.g., `assert Settings(environment='prod').resolved_log_level == 'INFO'`) so the assertion exercises actual code behaviour.
